name: MV cable schedules — sizing, derating, routing, terminations
standard: IEC 60502-1/-2:2014 (Power cables 1 – 30 kV) + IEC 60364-7-712:2017 (PV installations) + IEC 60287 series (current rating) + IEEE 525-2016 (Cable systems in substations) + IEEE 835-1994 (Power cable ampacity)
description: MV cable schedule rule pack for utility-scale renewables EPC (PV array feeders to MV transformers + collector-circuit to POI substation, typical 33 kV / 34.5 kV EU/US, 22 kV common in Asia). Validates ampacity vs aggregated array / turbine current including future inverter clipping; voltage drop ≤ 3 % (often ≤ 2 % per IEC 60364 recommendation for PV feeders); short-circuit withstand sized for fault current × clearing time; derating factors applied (depth of burial per IEC 60287, soil thermal resistivity ρ_t in K·m/W, grouping factor, ambient temperature 20 °C reference, direct-buried vs duct vs cable tray); conduit fill ≤ 40 % per NEC 300.17 if US install; trench depth meets local code (typically 0.7 m unprotected / 0.5 m with protection per EN 50522 + national code); joint + termination kit specified per cable type (XLPE / EPR), termination class per IEC 60502 indoor / outdoor; thermomechanical bonding documented for long runs.
